questions.question_text: Many patients 'break down' when confronted with their deepest fears. What does 'break down' mean?

options.text: To become calm and handle a difficult situation with ease
To lose emotional control and become overwhelmed by distress
To solve and overcome a difficult problem with confidence
To retreat and quietly reflect on a situation alone

questions.explanation_ar: break down تعني ينهار عاطفياً ويفقد السيطرة، أي الانهيار تحت وطأة الضغط النفسي الشديد.

questions.question_text: Fear can significantly 'impact' someone's quality of life. What does 'impact' mean?

options.text: To have no effect on something at all
To have a noticeable and significant effect on something
To gradually remove something from a situation over time
To slowly improve something through careful management

questions.explanation_ar: impact تعني يُؤثّر ويُحدث أثراً ملحوظاً، أي أن الشيء يُفضي إلى تغيير واضح في حالة ما أو نتيجة ما.

questions.question_text: VRET can 'manipulate' the virtual environment to adjust the intensity of fear exposure. What does 'manipulate' mean here?

options.text: To destroy and permanently delete parts of a virtual scene
To control and adjust elements in a skilful and precise way
To copy and duplicate an existing environment without changes
To ignore and bypass the settings in a virtual programme

questions.explanation_ar: manipulate تعني يُعدّل ويتحكم بمهارة، أي ضبط عناصر البيئة الافتراضية بطريقة دقيقة ومتحكّم بها وفق احتياجات المريض.

questions.question_text: Dr. Al-Mansouri emphasises that her approach 'aligns' with cultural values of dignity. What does 'aligns' mean?

options.text: Conflicts with and contradicts existing values and beliefs
Fits perfectly and is consistent with something else
Ignores and disregards the values of a particular culture
Replaces and eliminates outdated cultural practices

questions.explanation_ar: aligns تعني يتوافق ويتوافق مع، أي أن الأسلوب العلاجي يتّسق ويتناغم مع القيم الثقافية بدلاً من أن يتعارض معها.

questions.question_text: Researchers who 'persist' through fear-inducing challenges experience lasting brain changes. What does 'persist' mean?

options.text: To give up when a task becomes too challenging to continue
To take a long break before deciding to continue a task
To continue trying despite difficulties, without giving up
To change direction and try a completely different approach

questions.explanation_ar: persist تعني يُثابر ويواصل رغم الصعاب، أي الاستمرار في المحاولة دون الاستسلام حتى تحقيق الهدف.

questions.question_text: An 'obstacle' to personal growth is something that helps and makes progress easier to achieve.
questions.explanation_ar: خطأ. obstacle تعني عقبة وحاجز، أي شيء يُصعّب التقدم ويمنع تحقيق الهدف — وهو عكس المساعدة والتيسير.

questions.question_text: When someone has learned to 'cope with' their anxiety, they have developed strategies to manage and live with it.
questions.explanation_ar: صحيح. cope with تعني يتعامل مع ويتكيّف مع، أي تطوير أساليب عملية للتعايش مع الضغوط أو المشكلات دون أن تؤثر سلباً على الحياة.

questions.question_text: People who avoid facing fears tend to _____ on them, which actually strengthens the fear over time.
questions.explanation_ar: dwell (on) تعني يتمحور حول ويفكر مراراً في، وهذا التفكير المتكرر يُعزّز المسارات العصبية للخوف ويجعله أشد.

questions.question_text: Which sentence uses a reporting verb (not 'say' or 'tell') correctly?

options.text: She explained that VR had reduced her patients' anxiety significantly.
She explained her patients that VR had reduced anxiety significantly.
She noted to her patients that VR would reduces anxiety.
She emphasised her patients that healing requires human connection.

questions.explanation_ar: explain (like say) لا يحتاج مفعولاً به مباشراً قبل that. الخيار a صحيح، بينما الخيار b يخطئ بإضافة 'her patients' مباشرة بعد explained.

questions.question_text: Convert: Dr. Al-Mansouri told the patient, 'Do not avoid situations that frighten you.'

options.text: Dr. Al-Mansouri told the patient do not avoid situations.
Dr. Al-Mansouri told the patient not to avoid situations that frightened her.
Dr. Al-Mansouri said the patient not avoid situations.
Dr. Al-Mansouri told the patient that she should not avoiding situations.

questions.explanation_ar: الأوامر المنقولة: told + person + (not) + to + infinitive. الخيار b صحيح: told the patient not to avoid...

questions.question_text: Convert: 'I have not finished the therapy programme,' the patient said.

options.text: The patient said that he has not finished the therapy programme.
The patient said that he had not finished the therapy programme.
The patient told that he had not finished the therapy programme.
The patient said that he not had finished the therapy programme.

questions.explanation_ar: في الكلام المنقول: المضارع التام (have not finished) → ماضٍ تام (had not finished). الخيار b صحيح. الخيار c خاطئ (told بدون مفعول به).

questions.question_text: Which sentence uses a reporting verb correctly to describe how something was said?

options.text: She noted that the technology was still being tested.
She noted her students that the technology was still being tested.
She emphasised to that progress had been remarkable.
She clarified us that the results were very promising.

questions.explanation_ar: note (كـ say) لا يأخذ مفعولاً به مباشراً قبل that. الخيار a صحيح. الخيار b يضيف 'her students' خطأً، والخيار d يستخدم 'us' بدلاً من 'us that' بطريقة غير صحيحة.

questions.question_text: 'The doctor told his patient not to dwell on past failures' is a correct reported command.
questions.explanation_ar: صحيح. told + person + not to + infinitive هي الصيغة الصحيحة للأمر السلبي المنقول. الجملة صحيحة تماماً.

questions.question_text: Match each reporting verb to the type of statement it introduces.

right_items: confessed to something negative or difficult
made something clear with reasons or details
said strongly that something was not true
told someone about a possible danger

correct_answer.right: made something clear with reasons or details
told someone about a possible danger
said strongly that something was not true
confessed to something negative or difficult

questions.explanation_ar: أفعال الكلام المتنوعة: explained (يُوضّح)، warned (يُحذّر)، denied (يُنكر)، admitted (يعترف). كل فعل يُحدد طبيعة الكلام المُنقَل وأسلوبه.

questions.question_text: According to Passage B, what percentage of patients showed improvement with traditional therapy (without VR)?
questions.explanation_ar: ذكر النص أن 67% من المرضى الذين خضعوا للعلاج التقليدي دون واقع افتراضي تحسّنوا، مقارنةً بـ 83% من مرضى VRET.

questions.question_text: According to Passage B, after how many VRET sessions did brain imaging show decreased amygdala activity?

options.text: After 4 sessions
After 6 sessions
After 8 sessions
After 12 sessions

questions.explanation_ar: كشفت دراسات التصوير الدماغي وفق ما ذكره النص أن المرضى يُظهرون انخفاضاً في نشاط اللوزة الدماغية بعد ثماني جلسات من العلاج بالتعرض الافتراضي.

questions.question_text: Passage A says fear 'can both protect and hinder us.' What does this tell us about the nature of fear?

options.text: Fear is purely negative and should be completely eliminated
Fear is an emotion that has both useful and harmful sides
Fear only affects people in physical danger, not in daily life
Fear becomes useful only when managed by professional therapists

questions.explanation_ar: يُضمّن النص أن الخوف له وجهان: وجه إيجابي كآلية وقاية، ووجه سلبي حين يُقيّد الإنسان ويُعيق أهدافه — مما يجعله عاطفة ذات طابع مزدوج.

questions.question_text: According to Passage B, VRET produces brain changes similar to real-world exposure but with significantly reduced risk of psychological trauma.
questions.explanation_ar: صحيح. اكتشف الباحثون وفق النص أن VRET يُغيّر الأنماط الدماغية بطريقة مشابهة للتعرض الحقيقي لكن مع تقليل كبير لخطر الصدمة النفسية.

questions.question_text: According to Passage B, brain imaging showed decreased amygdala activity after just _____ VRET sessions.
questions.explanation_ar: ذكر النص أن دراسات التصوير الدماغي كشفت عن انخفاض في نشاط اللوزة الدماغية بعد ثماني جلسات فقط من العلاج بالتعرض الافتراضي.

questions.question_text: Match each concept from both passages to its correct explanation.

right_items: 83% improvement rate versus 67% — showing VR's superiority
brain region responsible for processing fear responses
system causing racing heart, tense muscles, and shallow breathing
technique of slowly introducing feared situations in a controlled way

correct_answer.right: brain region responsible for processing fear responses
system causing racing heart, tense muscles, and shallow breathing
technique of slowly introducing feared situations in a controlled way
83% improvement rate versus 67% — showing VR's superiority

questions.explanation_ar: ورد كل مفهوم في النصين مع وصف وظيفته: اللوزة (استجابة الخوف)، دائرة الخوف (أعراض جسدية)، التعرض التدريجي (أسلوب علاجي)، ومقارنة VRET بالعلاج التقليدي.
